color_profiles.name: Colorless (D–F on GIA scale)
color_profiles.cause: structural purity; absence of nitrogen and other chromophores

color_profiles.name: Cape Yellow / Fancy Yellow
color_profiles.cause: nitrogen impurities substituting for carbon (Type Ia / Type Ib)

color_profiles.name: Brown Diamond
color_profiles.cause: plastic deformation and vacancy clusters along crystal planes

color_profiles.name: Pink / Red Diamond
color_profiles.variety_name: Argyle Pink, Fancy Pink, Red Diamond
color_profiles.cause: structural defects / plastic deformation (precise mechanism still debated)

color_profiles.name: Green Diamond
color_profiles.cause: natural radiation exposure (alpha particle irradiation of crystal lattice)

color_profiles.name: Black Diamond (Carbonado)
color_profiles.cause: polycrystalline aggregate with inclusions of graphite, amorphous carbon, and sulfides

textures: smooth adamantine luster on fresh crystal faces
frosted or etched on alluvial grains
trigon etch pits on octahedral faces
visual_notes: Unmistakable adamantine luster and exceptional brilliance in cut stones. Rough diamonds often appear greasy or frosted. Dispersion produces fire — flashes of spectral color — unmatched by any other colorless gemstone except moissanite.

optical_effects.fluorescence: blue fluorescence common (~25–35% of gem diamonds) under shortwave UV; some fluoresce yellow, orange, or green; many inert
